title: May 16, 2009: The day Nadal and Djokovic played the longest three-set match in ATP Masters 1000 history
description: Each day, Tennis Majors takes you back in time to relive a tennis event which happened on this specific day. On this day in 2009, Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic played the longest three-set match in ATP Masters 1000 history in the semi-finals of the Madrid Masters 1000

title: Rain halts Rome semi-final summit, Sinner’s hardest match since February
description: Jannik Sinner (No 1) leads Daniil Medvedev (No 7) 6-2, 5-7, 4-2 in a suspended Rome semi-final, the Italian two games from the final but visibly tired after a medical timeout at 3-2 in the third. The Russian had made it 5-7, taking the first set off Sinner this tournament. Play resumes Saturday from 3 p.m. local time.

title: “The most beautiful tournament of my life”: exausted Darderi dedicates his Rome run to his family
description: "I had no fuel today." Luciano Darderi apologised to the crowd after a 6-1, 6-1 semi-final loss to Casper Ruud, at the end of a week that included his first top-three win, over Zverev.

title: Rome makes four: Ruud completes the big clay-court finals set with Darderi rout
description: Casper Ruud (No 13) crushed Luciano Darderi (No 18) 6-1, 6-1 in 66 minutes on Campo Centrale to reach his first Rome final and complete the clay-court Masters 1000 set — Monte-Carlo, Madrid, Rome and Roland-Garros. Sinner or Medvedev awaits on Sunday.

title: Espresso’s Not Enough to Help Darderi Against Ruud in Rome

title: “Just couldn’t dream about a better year”: Svitolina’s post-maternity comeback reaches a Rome final

title: Gauff reaches back-to-back Rome finals with straight-sets win over Cirstea
description: Coco Gauff (No 4) reached a second straight Rome final with a 6-4, 6-3 win over Sorana Cirstea (No 26), ending a run of three consecutive three-set wins in the Italian capital. It is the American's seventh WTA 1000 final at 22 – and her second of 2026 after Miami.
